title: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — SSP Auction Requests After “Limit Use of Sensitive PI” Flag

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.121 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.121 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.22: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — SSP Auction Requests After “Limit Use of Sensitive PI” Flag.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.22' running on sourcetype prebid:auction and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=ads` `sourcetype="prebid:auction"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use, segments, _time); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use as consent_flag, updated_ts)
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" limit_sensitive_ad_use=true earliest=-30d
| eval eff=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left user_id [
    search index=ads sourcetype="prebid:auction" earliest=-30d
    | eval has_sensitive=if(match(segments,"(?i)(health|finance|precise_geo)"),1,0)
    | rename _time as auction_time
    | fields user_id, auction_time, has_sensitive, segments
  ]
| where auction_time>eff AND has_sensitive=1
| stats count by user_id
| sort - count
description: Limiting use of sensitive PI for advertising must be operationally enforced. Programmatic auction requests that still attach sensitive audience segments after the limit flag is set indicate pipeline defects with high regulatory salience.
value: Limiting use of sensitive PI for advertising must be operationally enforced. Programmatic auction requests that still attach sensitive audience segments after the limit flag is set indicate pipeline defects with high regulatory salience.
implementation: (1) Legal defines `segments` taxonomy; (2) strip segment contents in long-term storage—hash or bucketize for Splunk; (3) block supply in ad server when possible; (4) daily compliance digest; (5) vendor notification workflow.

grandmaExplanation: Limiting use of sensitive PI for advertising must be operationally enforced — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Prebid/SSP server logs via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ads` `sourcetype="prebid:auction"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use, segments, _time); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use as consent_flag, updated_ts).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Legal defines `segments` taxonomy; (2) strip segment contents in long-term storage—hash or bucketize for Splunk; (3) block supply in ad server when possible; (4) daily compliance digest; (5) vendor notification workflow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:state" limit_sensitive_ad_use=true earliest=-30d
| eval eff=strptime(updated_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left user_id [
    search index=ads sourcetype="prebid:auction" earliest=-30d
    | eval has_sensitive=if(match(segments,"(?i)(health|finance|precise_geo)"),1,0)
    | rename _time as auction_time
    | fields user_id, auction_time, has_sensitive, segments
  ]
| where auction_time>eff AND has_sensitive=1
| stats count by user_id
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — SSP Auction Requests After “Limit Use of Sensitive PI” Flag** — Limiting use of sensitive PI for advertising must be operationally enforced. Programmatic auction requests that still attach sensitive audience segments after the limit flag is set indicate pipeline defects with high regulatory salience.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ads` `sourcetype="prebid:auction"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use, segments, _time); `index=privacy` `sourcetype="consent:state"` (user_id, limit_sensitive_ad_use as consent_flag, updated_ts). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Prebid/SSP server logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: consent:state. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="consent:state", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **eff** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Filters the current rows with `where auction_time>eff AND has_sensitive=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (users), Single value (auction violations), Timeline.
